metadata.title: Instrument Cluster — CAN Communication Function System Requirements Specification
metadata.author: Systems Engineering Team

requirements.title: HS-CAN physical layer compliance
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL implement a high-speed CAN physical layer compliant with ISO 11898-2 on the powertrain bus and the body bus, operating at a nominal bit rate of 500 kbit/s. The cluster SHALL maintain differential signal levels within the ISO 11898-2 recessive (0.5 V–2.25 V) and dominant (1.5 V–3.0 V) voltage ranges under all specified operating temperatures.
requirements.rationale: ISO 11898-2 physical layer compliance is the vehicle platform standard for all ECUs on the powertrain and body networks, as defined in SYS-PLAT-NET-001. Physical layer conformance is a prerequisite for all higher-layer communication requirements in this specification.

requirements.title: CAN FD backbone interface
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL implement a CAN FD interface compliant with ISO 11898-1:2015 on the vehicle backbone bus, with an arbitration phase bit rate of 500 kbit/s and a data phase bit rate of 2 Mbit/s. The cluster SHALL support CAN FD frames with data fields up to 64 bytes. Bit rate switching SHALL be activated only after successful arbitration.
requirements.rationale: CAN FD is the platform standard for the backbone bus on this vehicle segment, enabling large-payload OTA metadata exchange and high-frequency signal transmission without bandwidth limitation. Defined in SYS-PLAT-NET-002.

requirements.title: E2E protection for safety-relevant received signals
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL apply AUTOSAR E2E Profile 2 verification to all CAN messages carrying ASIL-tagged signals, including vehicle speed (from ABS/ESC ECU), engine status, airbag system status, and brake system warning. The cluster SHALL verify the E2E CRC and message counter on each received frame. A frame failing E2E verification SHALL be discarded; if three consecutive frames fail E2E verification, the corresponding signal SHALL be declared invalid and the cluster SHALL enter the signal-loss handling state defined in SYS-IC-CAN-004.
requirements.rationale: Without E2E integrity protection, single-bit transmission errors or message substitution on the CAN bus could cause the cluster to display an incorrect safety-critical parameter value without indication of a fault (hazardous event HA-IC-CAN-001: display of erroneous safety signal value). E2E Profile 2 is the AUTOSAR-recommended profile for ASIL-B signals over CAN. Three-consecutive-frame threshold prevents nuisance faults from isolated bit errors.

requirements.title: Safety-relevant signal reception timeout monitoring
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL monitor the reception interval of every ASIL-tagged CAN signal. If a signal is not received within 200% of its nominal transmission cycle time (as specified in the vehicle communication matrix CAN-DBC-001), the signal SHALL be declared lost. On signal loss, the cluster SHALL: (1) replace the displayed value with a defined failure indicator (dashes or warning symbol as specified per signal in ICD-IC-HMI-001); (2) illuminate the relevant system communication fault telltale; (3) store a DTC identifying the missing transmitter node.
requirements.rationale: Silent loss of a safety-relevant CAN signal (e.g., ABS status, vehicle speed) without driver notification constitutes hazardous event HA-IC-CAN-001. The 200% timeout threshold tolerates one missed transmission cycle before declaring loss, preventing nuisance faults during legitimate bus load peaks. Failure indicator content is governed by ICD-IC-HMI-001 to ensure ISO 2575 symbol compliance.

requirements.title: CAN bus-off detection and automatic recovery
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL detect a CAN controller bus-off condition (transmit error counter exceeding 255 per ISO 11898-1) within one error-passive cycle. On bus-off entry, the cluster SHALL: (1) log the bus-off event with timestamp to NVM; (2) execute automatic recovery by waiting the ISO 11898-1 mandated 128 x 11-bit recessive period; (3) re-attempt bus integration up to three times within a 5-second window. If bus-off persists beyond three recovery attempts, the affected CAN channel SHALL be isolated and all signals previously received on that channel SHALL be treated as lost per SYS-IC-CAN-004.
requirements.rationale: CAN bus-off causes total loss of all signals on the affected bus, including safety-relevant signals. Automatic recovery minimises display blackout duration. Three-attempt limit with channel isolation prevents the cluster from continuously flooding a faulty bus with error frames, which would impair other nodes (HA-IC-CAN-002: total loss of cluster display data). NVM logging supports post-event diagnostic root cause analysis.

requirements.title: Vehicle speed signal cross-plausibility check
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL perform a plausibility cross-check between the vehicle speed signal received from the ABS/ESC ECU (primary source) and the vehicle speed derived from the engine ECU torque and gear ratio signals (secondary source). If the two speed values deviate by more than 15 km/h for a period exceeding 1000 ms, the cluster SHALL suppress the primary speed display, show a designated plausibility fault indicator, and set DTC U0121 (Lost Communication with ABS Control Module).
requirements.rationale: E2E protection (SYS-IC-CAN-003) defends against transmission errors; plausibility checking defends against systematic faults in the transmitting ECU that produce a valid-CRC but physically implausible speed value. Cross-source plausibility is a Defence-in-Depth measure. 15 km/h threshold is derived from the maximum legitimate instantaneous speed difference between signal sources under normal driving conditions (gear change, wheel slip events).

requirements.title: CAN network management — AUTOSAR NM sleep and wake coordination
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL participate in AUTOSAR Network Management (NM) bus sleep coordination on all connected CAN networks. The cluster SHALL transmit NM PDUs at the configured NM message cycle time and SHALL vote for bus sleep only after confirming that no locally required application signals are outstanding. The cluster SHALL NOT hold the network awake beyond its own KL15-off shutdown sequence as defined in SYS-IC-PWR-006.
requirements.rationale: Failure to participate correctly in AUTOSAR NM would either prevent bus sleep (excessive dark current, battery drain) or cause premature sleep that interrupts other nodes' communication. Cluster NM participation is governed by the vehicle network management specification SYS-PLAT-NM-001.

requirements.title: Diagnostic CAN channel — ISO 15765-2 transport layer and UDS
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL implement the ISO 15765-2 (CAN transport layer) and ISO 14229-1 (UDS — Unified Diagnostic Services) protocol stack on the dedicated diagnostic CAN channel at 500 kbit/s. The cluster SHALL respond to physical and functional addressed diagnostic requests using the ECU address defined in the vehicle diagnostic address allocation matrix DIAG-ADDR-001. Supported UDS services SHALL include as a minimum: 0x10 (DiagnosticSessionControl), 0x11 (ECUReset), 0x19 (ReadDTCInformation), 0x22 (ReadDataByIdentifier), 0x27 (SecurityAccess), 0x2E (WriteDataByIdentifier), 0x34/0x36/0x37 (firmware download services).
requirements.rationale: UDS over CAN is the mandated diagnostic protocol for all ECUs on this vehicle platform per SYS-PLAT-DIAG-001, ensuring compatibility with OEM and workshop diagnostic tools. ISO 14229-1 service set is the minimum required for OBD-II legislation compliance and software update capability.

requirements.title: UDS security access control for reprogramming sessions
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL require successful completion of UDS SecurityAccess (service 0x27, security level 0x03) seed-and-key authentication before permitting entry into the UDS programmingSession (service 0x10 subfunction 0x02) or execution of firmware download services (0x34, 0x36, 0x37). The seed SHALL be a cryptographically random 8-byte value generated using the cluster hardware RNG. The expected key SHALL be derived from the seed using HMAC-SHA256 with a device-unique secret stored in the cluster secure element. Failed authentication attempts SHALL be counted; three consecutive failures SHALL lock the security access for a minimum of 10 seconds.
requirements.rationale: Unrestricted access to firmware reprogramming via the OBD-II diagnostic port is a primary physical attack vector for ECU compromise (TARA-IC asset: cluster firmware integrity; threat: unauthorized firmware replacement via OBD-II port; risk level: HIGH; cybersecurity goal CG-IC-001). HMAC-SHA256 with device-unique key prevents a captured seed-key pair from being reused across devices. Brute-force lockout limits offline guessing attacks.

requirements.title: SecOC message authentication for safety-relevant CAN signals
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL verify AUTOSAR SecOC (Secure Onboard Communication) Message Authentication Codes (MAC) on all CAN messages designated as cybersecurity-relevant in the vehicle communication matrix CAN-DBC-001. MAC verification SHALL use CMAC-AES-128 with a session key derived per the vehicle key management specification SYS-PLAT-KM-001. A received CAN message with an invalid or absent MAC SHALL be silently discarded; if three consecutive messages from a single transmitter fail MAC verification, the cluster SHALL log DTC U3001 (SecOC verification failure) and treat the signal as lost per SYS-IC-CAN-004.
requirements.rationale: CAN bus message injection is feasible via physical access to the OBD-II port or a compromised gateway ECU. Without SecOC, an attacker could inject spoofed vehicle speed, gear position, or warning indicator values causing the cluster to display misleading safety information (TARA-IC threat: CAN signal spoofing to falsify displayed safety state; cybersecurity goal CG-IC-002). SecOC supplements E2E protection (SYS-IC-CAN-003): E2E detects accidental bit errors; SecOC detects intentional injection.

requirements.title: CAN message reception jitter tolerance
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL accept and correctly process cyclic CAN messages that arrive with a timing jitter of up to ±15% of their nominal transmission cycle time without triggering a false timeout or E2E counter fault. The cluster CAN stack SHALL implement a reception jitter buffer sized to accommodate the maximum expected network load of 60% bus utilisation at 500 kbit/s.
requirements.rationale: CAN bus arbitration and variable network load introduce transmission jitter on all cyclic messages. An overly strict reception window would generate false timeout faults (spurious telltale activations) under normal high-load driving conditions. The ±15% tolerance and 60% bus load figure are derived from network load analysis report NET-LOAD-IC-001.

requirements.title: CAN error passive state detection and channel isolation
requirements.text: The instrument cluster CAN communication function SHALL monitor its CAN controller error counters on all active bus channels. If a channel enters error-passive state (transmit or receive error counter exceeding 127 per ISO 11898-1) and remains in that state for more than 5000 ms without recovery, the cluster SHALL isolate the affected channel, treat all signals previously sourced from that channel as lost per SYS-IC-CAN-004, and store a DTC identifying the affected network. Channel isolation SHALL prevent the cluster CAN controller from contributing further error frames to the bus.
requirements.rationale: An ECU in error-passive state can continue to transmit error frames at a reduced rate, contributing to overall bus error load and potentially degrading other nodes' communication performance. Prolonged error-passive without recovery indicates a persistent physical or protocol fault requiring isolation. ASIL-A assigned per HARA HA-IC-CAN-002 (cluster-generated error frames impairing transmission of safety signals from other nodes).
